notes: SPIN Evaluation Protocol — deterministic, multi-layer German language analysis benchmark. SPIN's thesis: better results with fewer rules and fundamentally different approaches (phonotactics, clause analysis, structural diagnosis). The benchmark measures this claim honestly.

components.description: Grammar rule detection accuracy — precision and recall of GR_RULES against annotated German text.
Clause detection accuracy — correct identification of sentence topology (simple/compound/complex/compound-complex).
Phonotactic analysis quality — detection of illegal bigrams, sonority violations, syllable weight anomalies.
Replay stability — identical outputs on identical inputs across runs.
Runtime performance — throughput, memory usage, processing time per example.
Slice reporting — performance breakdown across error categories, input lengths, and difficulty levels.
Approach quality — true positives per active rule, layer contribution analysis, rule utilization rate. Measures the 'fewer rules, better results' thesis.

layers: phonotactics — phonological constraint validation (SSP, feature geometry)
evaluation_method.description: All evaluations use deterministic multi-layer analysis with no stochastic components. Four analysis layers work together to achieve high precision with minimal rules.
evaluation_method.comparison_baseline: LanguageTool DE categories (~3000+ rules, single-layer pattern matching)
unique_differentiators: Multi-layer analysis (4 layers vs. single-layer pattern matching)
Phonotactic analysis layer (SSP-based, feature-geometric — unique to SPIN)
Six-state diagnosis engine for sentence-level structural analysis
Deterministic by design — no randomness in evaluation
Precision-first approach — fewer rules, near-zero false positives
